name: National Foundation for Credit Counseling, Inc.
description_short: NFCC is a nonprofit credit counseling network offering free and low-cost financial guidance through 1,500+ certified counselors to help consumers manage debt and achieve financial wellness.
description_long:
The National Foundation For Credit Counseling (NFCC) was established in 1951 and has grown into a trusted network of nonprofit credit counseling agencies. Operating through member agencies across the country, NFCC has served approximately 35 million people since 2006, positioning itself as one of the largest and most established nonprofit credit counseling organizations in the United States. The organization is built on a mission to provide safe, affordable financial guidance to consumers struggling with debt.

NFCC offers comprehensive credit counseling services including credit card debt management, budgeting and financial planning, homeowner guidance and foreclosure prevention, student loan debt counseling, reverse mortgage counseling, renter budgeting, bankruptcy guidance, and small business owner finances. Their primary tool is the Debt Management Plan (DMP), which consolidates multiple debts into a single monthly payment with the goal of reducing interest rates and stopping collection calls. All services are delivered through one-on-one confidential consultations with NFCC-certified credit counselors, followed by personalized financial action plans. Services are available via phone (800-388-2227), video chat, and in-person at member agencies.

The NFCC distinguishes itself through its nonprofit structure, eliminating profit-driven incentives that plague the credit counseling industry. The organization maintains strict certification standards for its counselors and has been operating since 1951, demonstrating institutional longevity and regulatory compliance. Their extensive network of 1,500+ certified counselors provides geographic accessibility, and they explicitly advertise that using their debt analysis tool will not affect credit scores—a critical distinction from debt settlement or credit repair approaches.

A significant caveat is that NFCC is a network/umbrella organization, not a direct service provider. Actual services are delivered through member agencies (Money Management International, Apprisen, InCharge Debt Solutions, GreenPath Financial Wellness, etc.), meaning quality and experience may vary. While the website emphasizes affordability and "no hidden fees," specific pricing details for services are not disclosed on the main website. The organization's reliance on a Debt Management Plan as a primary solution may not address all debt situations, particularly those requiring bankruptcy or more aggressive settlement strategies.
pros: 1,500+ NFCC-certified credit counselors across a national network of member agencies
Established nonprofit structure since 1951 with 35 million people served since 2006
Debt Management Plans offer lower interest rates, consolidated single monthly payments, and promise to stop collection calls
Confidential one-on-one consultations with personalized financial action plans tailored to individual situations
Multiple service channels: phone (800-388-2227), video chat, and in-person consultations at member agencies
Wide range of specialized counseling services including foreclosure prevention, reverse mortgage counseling, and bankruptcy guidance
Explicitly states that using their debt analysis tools will not affect credit scores
cons: NFCC is a network organization—actual services delivered by member agencies with potentially inconsistent quality across different providers
Specific pricing and fee structures not disclosed on website; consumers must contact to learn actual costs despite 'affordable' claims
Limited detail on Debt Management Plan success rates, typical interest rate reductions, or timeline expectations
Website does not clearly explain what disqualifies someone from services or when alternatives (bankruptcy, debt settlement) might be more appropriate
Testimonials featured are from specific member agencies, not necessarily representative of full NFCC network experience
best_for: Consumers with multiple credit card debts seeking affordable debt consolidation without profit-driven sales pressure
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need HUD-approved guidance and negotiation support with lenders
First-time homebuyers or current homeowners seeking educational guidance on mortgages and home protection
Individuals with student loan debt or those considering bankruptcy who need objective counseling before making major decisions
diagnosis: NFCC is best for consumers seeking legitimate, nonprofit credit counseling and debt management planning without profit-driven sales tactics. The main caveat is that NFCC operates as a network coordinator rather than direct service provider—actual quality depends on which member agency serves your area, and specific pricing and success metrics are not transparent on the website.
services: Credit card debt counseling and Debt Management Plans
Homeowner guidance and foreclosure prevention counseling
One-on-one financial consultations with certified counselors
Online financial education courses and FAQs
Debt analysis tools to estimate payoff options
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2033 K St Nw, Suite 425, Washington, DC 20006
meta_description: NFCC offers free and low-cost credit counseling through 1,500+ certified counselors to help consumers manage debt and achieve financial wellness.
